relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: On the third day, a wedding opens the sign-story in Cana.
Jesus and his disciples are invited into celebration.
The mother of Jesus sees the lack before anyone else speaks it.
Jesus names the mystery of timing: his hour has not yet come.
His mother turns attention to obedience: do whatever he says.
Stone jars of purification wait as vessels of transformation.
Water fills the vessels to the top.
The drawn water is carried into the feast.
The steward tastes transformation without knowing its source; the servants know.
The beautiful wine has been kept until now.
The first sign reveals glory and awakens trust.
Family and disciples remain together in Capernaum for a few days.
Passover draws Jesus up to Jerusalem.
The temple is found filled with buying and selling.
Jesus disrupts the marketplace inside sacred space.
The Father’s house is not to become a trading house.
The disciples remember zeal for the house.
The leaders ask for a sign to authorize the action.
Jesus gives the temple-body sign of three-day raising.
They hear only the building and miss the body.
The true temple he speaks of is his body.
After resurrection, memory opens Scripture and spoken word into trust.
Many trust because of signs.
Jesus knows sign-trust is not yet full entrusting.
He knows what is in the human without needing testimony.
